techniques.evidence_quote: two former workers at its e-commerce provider GoQuo in a development center in India "improperly accessed and stole the personal data of our customers."
two former employees of a third-party provider were responsible for a massive breach
Initial reports suggested a misconfigured Amazon Web Services (AWS) S3 bucket may have been to blame
improperly accessed and stole the personal data of our customers
spotted circulating on the dark web

attack_chain.description: Two former employees of Malindo Air's third-party e-commerce provider GoQuo (at a development center in India) leveraged their trusted third-party access to the airline's customer data environment.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: two former workers at its e-commerce provider GoQuo in a development center in India "improperly accessed and stole the personal data of our customers."

attack_chain.description: The former GoQuo employees used valid account credentials they retained from their prior employment to authenticate to systems storing Malindo Air / Thai Lion Air passenger data.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: two former employees of a third-party provider were responsible for a massive breach

evidence_chunks.quote: two former employees of a third-party provider were responsible for a massive breach
two former employees of a third-party provider were responsible for a massive breach

attack_chain.description: Accessed passenger PII stored in cloud storage (initially suspected to be an AWS S3 bucket, though AWS confirmed its services were not compromised). Records included names, DOBs, phone numbers, emails, addresses, and passport numbers/expiration dates.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Initial reports suggested a misconfigured Amazon Web Services (AWS) S3 bucket may have been to blame

evidence_chunks.quote: Initial reports suggested a misconfigured Amazon Web Services (AWS) S3 bucket may have been to blame
Initial reports suggested a misconfigured Amazon Web Services (AWS) S3 bucket may have been to blame

attack_chain.description: Stole approximately 35 million customer PII records belonging to Malindo Air and Thai Lion Air passengers.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: improperly accessed and stole the personal data of our customers

evidence_chunks.quote: improperly accessed and stole the personal data of our customers
improperly accessed and stole the personal data of our customers

attack_chain.description: Stolen passenger records were leaked/traded on the dark web, monetizing the stolen PII.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: spotted circulating on the dark web last month

initial_access_vector: Two former employees of Malindo Air's third-party e-commerce provider GoQuo (India development center) abused retained access to improperly access and steal customer PII.
persistence_mechanism: Retained valid credentials/access from prior employment at third-party provider GoQuo after termination.
privilege_escalation: None identified; insiders already had legitimate access to customer data as part of their prior roles.
